front: What is a combinational logic circuit?
back: A digital circuit whose output is a pure function of the present input only. It has no internal memory and its output depends solely on the current combination of input values.

front: What is the purpose of a truth table in combinational logic?
back: A truth table is a systematic listing of all possible input combinations and their corresponding outputs. It defines the function of a combinational circuit completely and is used for analysis and design.

A combinational circuit that selects one of many input lines and directs it to a single output line. The selection is controlled by a set of select lines. It acts as a digitally controlled multi-position switch.

front: What is the difference between a decoder and an encoder?
back: A decoder converts a binary code on its n inputs into a one-hot output on 2^n outputs. An encoder performs the inverse function, converting a one-hot input on 2^n lines into a binary code on n outputs.

front: What is the fundamental goal of logic minimization?
back: To find the simplest Boolean expression or logic circuit that implements a specified function. This reduces cost, power consumption, and physical size while improving performance. Techniques include Karnaugh Maps and the Quine-McCluskey algorithm.
